enrichment.short_description: Product management software for building lean roadmaps, managing ideas, and tracking OKRs with AI-assisted feedback synthesis.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ProdPad is product management software combining roadmaps, idea management, feedback, and OKRs, with an AI CoPilot and a free trial.

features.title: Now-Next-Later roadmaps
features.description: Build lean, timeline-free roadmaps using the Now-Next-Later framework invented by ProdPad's founders.

features.title: Idea management
features.description: Capture, organize, and prioritize product ideas in a dedicated backlog separate from the roadmap.

features.title: Customer feedback hub
features.description: Collect feedback from customers and internal teams, then use AI to detect recurring patterns and themes.

features.title: OKRs and objectives
features.description: Track objectives and key results and connect them directly to roadmap initiatives.

features.title: Product portfolio management
features.description: Manage multiple products or roadmaps together for organizations with more than one product line.

features.title: ProdPad CoPilot
features.description: An AI assistant that helps write roadmap content, prioritize ideas, and summarize customer feedback.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects to delivery and collaboration tools including Jira, Azure DevOps, GitHub, Trello, Slack, Salesforce, Intercom, Linear, Confluence, and Microsoft Teams.

pros: Invented the widely used Now-Next-Later roadmap format
Built by the founders of Mind the Product, giving it deep roots in the PM community
Free trial requires no credit card and can be extended by completing onboarding tasks
Unlimited free 'Reviewer' accounts let stakeholders view and comment without a paid seat
Broad set of integrations with delivery tools like Jira and Azure DevOps
cons: Specific plan names and per-seat pricing are not published; you must start a trial or contact sales to see pricing
Reviewer accounts have limited permissions compared to full paid seats

faqs.question: Does ProdPad offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes. ProdPad offers a minimum 7-day free trial with no credit card required, and the trial can be extended beyond 30 days by completing certain tasks in the product.

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: ProdPad doesn't publish a permanent free tier for editors, but it offers unlimited free 'Reviewer' accounts that can add and edit ideas and feedback with limited viewing permissions.

faqs.question: What is the Now-Next-Later roadmap?
faqs.answer: It's a roadmap framework invented by ProdPad's founders that organizes work into Now, Next, and Later horizons instead of fixed dates.

faqs.question: Does ProdPad have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes. ProdPad CoPilot is built into the product to help with writing, prioritization, organization, and synthesizing customer feedback.

faqs.question: What tools does ProdPad integrate with?
faqs.answer: ProdPad integrates with delivery and collaboration tools including Jira, Azure DevOps, GitHub, Trello, Slack, Salesforce, Intercom, Linear, Confluence, and Microsoft Teams.

use_cases.title: Lean roadmapping without fixed dates
use_cases.description: Product teams use the Now-Next-Later framework to communicate priorities without committing to exact delivery dates.

use_cases.title: Centralizing customer feedback
use_cases.description: Teams funnel feedback from multiple channels into ProdPad and use AI to spot recurring themes that inform the roadmap.

use_cases.title: Connecting OKRs to delivery
use_cases.description: Organizations track objectives and key results alongside the roadmap items meant to achieve them.

features: Access to all features during the trial

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ProdPad?
paragraphs: ProdPad is product management software that connects roadmaps, idea management, customer feedback, and OKRs in one system. It was created by Janna Bastow and Simon Cast, co-founders of Mind the Product, and popularized the Now-Next-Later roadmap format that many product teams use today.

The platform includes ProdPad CoPilot, an AI assistant that helps with writing roadmap content, prioritizing ideas, and summarizing customer feedback, alongside integrations with tools like Jira, Azure DevOps, and Slack.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ProdPad is for
paragraphs: ProdPad is aimed at product managers and product leaders who want to separate strategic roadmapping from delivery tracking, and who need a dedicated place to manage ideas and feedback before they become roadmap commitments.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered product management platform for prioritization, roadmapping, and OKRs, built by Lucid Software.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: airfocus is an AI-powered product management platform for prioritization, portfolio roadmaps, and OKRs, part of Lucid Software. No free trial.

features.title: AI-powered prioritization
features.description: Uses AI to help product teams prioritize initiatives and support decisions based on strategy, feedback, and roadmap data.

features.title: OKRs and objectives
features.description: Tracks objectives and key results alongside roadmap items.

features.title: Portfolio roadmaps
features.description: Visualizes roadmaps across multiple products with dependency tracking between initiatives.

features.title: Customer feedback and insights
features.description: Surfaces patterns automatically from incoming customer feedback.

features.title: Branded customer portal
features.description: Lets customers submit and track feedback through a portal branded to your company.

features.title: AI agents and MCP server
features.description: Includes AI agents for feedback triage and PRD co-authoring that work alongside tools like Claude and Copilot via an MCP server.

features.title: Risk and drift detection
features.description: Flags portfolio-level risks and blockers in real time.

pros: AI agents work alongside external tools like Claude and Copilot via an MCP server
Portfolio-level dependency tracking and risk/drift detection for complex organizations
EU-based hosting option is offered
Backed by Lucid Software, a larger visual collaboration company
Discounted pricing available for eligible startups, nonprofits, and educational organizations
cons: No free trial; evaluating the product requires booking a demo
Pricing for both plans (Professional and Enterprise) is not published and requires contacting sales
Advanced integrations (Jira Server, Azure DevOps Server, Salesforce) and SSO/SCIM are reserved for the Enterprise tier

faqs.question: Does airfocus offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No. airfocus states it does not offer a free trial, but prospective customers can book a personalized demo.

faqs.question: What plans does airfocus offer?
faqs.answer: airfocus offers a Professional plan and an Enterprise plan, both billed monthly or annually with pricing available on request.

faqs.question: Does airfocus include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes. airfocus includes an AI agent for tasks like feedback triage, an insights agent on the Enterprise plan, and an MCP server that lets it work with external AI tools like Claude and Copilot.

faqs.question: Is airfocus part of another company?
faqs.answer: Yes, airfocus is part of Lucid Software.

faqs.question: Does airfocus offer discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes, discounted pricing is available for eligible startups, nonprofits, and educational organizations.

use_cases.title: Portfolio-level product prioritization
use_cases.description: Product leaders use airfocus to prioritize initiatives across multiple products with AI-assisted decision support.

use_cases.title: Centralizing customer feedback
use_cases.description: Teams collect feedback through a branded customer portal and let AI surface recurring patterns.

use_cases.title: Tracking OKRs alongside delivery
use_cases.description: Enterprise customers connect OKRs and portfolio management to see how initiatives ladder up to strategic goals.

features: airfocus AI agent and MCP server
Jira Server, Azure DevOps Server, and Salesforce integrations
Portal SSO, IP whitelisting, SAML SSO, and SCIM provisioning

long_form_blocks.heading: What is airfocus?
paragraphs: airfocus is a product management platform, part of Lucid Software, that combines strategy, customer insights, and roadmapping with AI-assisted prioritization. It includes AI agents for feedback triage and PRD drafting, plus an MCP server that lets it connect to external AI tools like Claude and Copilot.
The platform is built for organizations managing multiple products, offering portfolio roadmaps with dependency tracking and automatic risk or drift detection across the portfolio.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who airfocus is for
paragraphs: airfocus targets product leaders and teams at larger, complex organizations that need portfolio-level visibility rather than a single team's roadmap. There is no free trial, so evaluation happens through a demo.

enrichment.short_description: Dragonboat is a product portfolio management platform using AI agents to align strategy, planning, and delivery across enterprise teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Dragonboat is an AI-powered product portfolio operating system for strategy, planning, and PDLC management, with Starter and Enterprise plans.

features.title: Portfolio operating system
features.description: Acts as a live semantic source of truth for product portfolio data, connecting strategy, intake, planning, and roadmap apps.

features.title: Strategies and Outcomes
features.description: Connects OKRs to roadmaps and tracks planned versus actual outcomes.

features.title: Headless API and MCP access
features.description: Provides a headless API and Model Context Protocol (MCP) access so AI agents can interact with portfolio data.

features.title: Ambient Agent
features.description: An AI agent that automatically tracks and reports across the product development lifecycle (PDLC), integrating with Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, and Slack.

features.title: Impact analysis
features.description: Runs real-time impact analysis to detect ripple effects and risk across the portfolio.

features.title: Customer insights
features.description: Centralizes customer insights to inform planning decisions.

pros: Includes a headless API and MCP server for connecting AI agents to portfolio data
Starter plan includes free read-only and requestor access for up to 100 users
SOC 2 certified with a stated policy that customer data is not used for external model training
Integrates with common delivery tools including Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, and Slack
cons: No pricing is published for either the Starter or Enterprise plan; both require contacting sales
No free trial is mentioned
Advanced capabilities like two-factor authentication, SSO, and a dedicated Customer Success Manager are reserved for the Enterprise tier

faqs.question: Does Dragonboat offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No free trial is mentioned on the pricing page; both the Starter and Enterprise plans require contacting sales for pricing.

faqs.question: What is included in the free access on Dragonboat's Starter plan?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan includes free read-only and requestor access for up to 100 users, alongside paid access for editors.

faqs.question: Does Dragonboat support AI agents?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dragonboat provides a headless API and MCP (Model Context Protocol) access so AI agents can read and act on portfolio data, plus its own Ambient Agent that tracks and reports across the PDLC.

faqs.question: Is Dragonboat SOC 2 certified?
faqs.answer: Yes, Dragonboat states it is SOC 2 certified and does not use customer data to train external models.

faqs.question: What integrations does Dragonboat support?
faqs.answer: Dragonboat integrates with Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, and Slack, with additional connectors available via API.

use_cases.title: Enterprise portfolio orchestration
use_cases.description: CPOs and product operations leaders use Dragonboat to get unified visibility across a large portfolio of products and initiatives.
use_cases.audience: Chief Product Officers and product operations leaders

use_cases.title: Connecting strategy to delivery
use_cases.description: Teams link OKRs to roadmap items and track planned versus actual outcomes over time.

use_cases.title: AI-agent-driven reporting
use_cases.description: Organizations use the Ambient Agent and API/MCP access to automate tracking and reporting across the PDLC.

features: Ambient Agent for auto-tracking and reporting across the PDLC
Integrations with Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, and Slack
Strategies and Outcomes connecting OKRs to roadmaps
Free read-only and requestor access for up to 100 users
Support for complex organizational structures and taxonomies
Portfolio-level insights with trend analysis and risk prediction

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Dragonboat?
paragraphs: Dragonboat is a product portfolio management platform that positions itself as an 'ontology-native operating system,' giving executives, product teams, and AI agents a shared, live view of strategy, investments, and product lifecycle data.

It includes built-in apps for strategy, intake, planning, and roadmapping, along with a headless API and MCP (Model Context Protocol) access that lets external AI agents read and act on portfolio data. An Ambient Agent automatically tracks and reports across the product development lifecycle.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Dragonboat is for
paragraphs: Dragonboat is built for enterprise organizations, particularly CPOs, CTOs, and product operations leaders who manage complex, multi-team product portfolios and want AI-assisted visibility across strategy and delivery.

enrichment.short_description: Craft.io is an end-to-end product management platform combining strategy, feedback, prioritization, roadmapping, and built-in Guru AI.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Craft.io is a product management platform with roadmapping, feedback, OKRs, and built-in Guru AI, starting at $19/editor/month billed annually.

features.title: Guru AI
features.description: Built-in AI that automates epic summarization, release note creation, and go-to-market brief preparation.

features.title: Roadmapping and backlog management
features.description: Combines visual roadmaps with backlog and feature definition tools.

features.title: Prioritization frameworks
features.description: Includes multiple built-in frameworks for prioritizing backlog items.

features.title: Feedback collection
features.description: Collects and organizes customer feedback alongside the product backlog.

features.title: OKRs and product strategy
features.description: Available as an add-on for tracking objectives and key results tied to roadmap execution.

features.title: Dependency management
features.description: Tracks dependencies between roadmap items on the Pro plan and above.

features.title: Design tool integrations
features.description: Connects to Figma, Adobe XD, Sketch, Mural, Axure, InVision, and Miro on the Pro plan and above.

features.title: Portfolio management
features.description: Enterprise plan supports multi-level OKRs, portfolio roadmaps, and cross-product dependencies.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required
Unlimited free contributor and viewer seats; only editors, team leaders, and admins require paid licenses
Guru AI is included even on the entry-level Starter plan
Broad integration list including Jira, Azure DevOps, GitHub, GitLab, Confluence, Figma, and Salesforce
cons: OKRs, feedback portal, and capacity planning are paid add-ons on the Starter and Pro plans rather than included
Slack and Microsoft Teams integrations were not yet available at the time of review
Enterprise pricing is custom and requires a demo

faqs.question: How much does Craft.io cost?
faqs.answer: Craft.io's Starter plan costs $24/editor/month billed monthly, or $19/editor/month billed annually. The Pro plan is $99/editor/month monthly or $79/editor/month annually. Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Does Craft.io offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Craft.io offers a 14-day free trial that doesn't require a credit card.

faqs.question: Are viewer and contributor seats free on Craft.io?
faqs.answer: Yes. Only editors, team leaders, and admins need a paid license; contributors and viewers are unlimited and free.

faqs.question: What AI features does Craft.io include?
faqs.answer: Craft.io includes Guru AI, which automates epic summaries, release notes, and go-to-market briefs, on all plans.

faqs.question: What's included in Craft.io's add-ons?
faqs.answer: OKRs and product strategy, the feedback portal, and capacity planning are available as add-ons for $20/month ($15/month billed annually) on the Starter and Pro plans, and are included by default on Enterprise.

use_cases.title: End-to-end product management
use_cases.description: Teams use Craft.io to move from strategy and feedback through prioritization and roadmapping in one platform.

use_cases.title: Portfolio and cross-team roadmaps
use_cases.description: Enterprise customers manage multi-level OKRs and cross-product dependencies across a product portfolio.
use_cases.audience: Chief Product Officers and product portfolio managers

use_cases.title: AI-assisted documentation
use_cases.description: Product teams use Guru AI to generate release notes and go-to-market briefs from existing product data.

features: Up to 3 workspace views and 3 personal views
Guru templates, views, and Guru AI
Unlimited workspaces, custom fields, and Guru views
Design tool integrations (Figma, Adobe XD, and others)
Slack, Microsoft Teams, and Salesforce integrations
Dedicated customer success manager and 24/7 priority support

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Craft.io?
paragraphs: Craft.io is an end-to-end product management platform covering strategy, feedback collection, prioritization, roadmapping, and execution in one workspace. It includes Guru AI, a built-in assistant for epic summaries, release notes, and go-to-market briefs.

Pricing is seat-based: only editors, team leaders, and admins require a paid license, while contributor and viewer seats are unlimited and free. Plans range from a $19-$24/editor/month Starter tier to custom Enterprise pricing with portfolio management and cross-product dependencies.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Craft.io is for
paragraphs: Craft.io is built for product managers, product owners, and product operations teams, scaling from small teams on the Starter plan up to Chief Product Officers managing multi-product portfolios on Enterprise.

enrichment.short_description: Roadmunk, now Tempo's Strategic Roadmaps, is a roadmapping platform for visualizing product strategy and tracking cross-team dependencies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Roadmunk (now Strategic Roadmaps by Tempo) offers roadmapping, idea management, and dependency tracking, starting at $19/editor/month annually.

features.title: Multiple roadmap views
features.description: Build roadmaps in timeline, swimlane, and table formats.

features.title: Idea Manager
features.description: Prioritize and manage a backlog of ideas separately from the roadmap.

features.title: Portfolio roadmaps
features.description: Aggregate multiple team roadmaps into one portfolio view on the Business plan and above.

features.title: Dependency tracking
features.description: Shows dependencies between projects across teams.

features.title: Customer feedback collection
features.description: Collects feedback and links it to roadmap items on the Business plan and above.

features.title: Two-way Jira/Azure DevOps sync
features.description: Optional add-on on Business, included on Professional, for two-way syncing with Jira and Azure DevOps.

pros: 14-day free trial with access to Business-tier features, including native Jira integration
ISO/IEC 27001 and 27701 certified, with GDPR and AICPA SOC compliance
Wide integration list: Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, Monday.com, Pivotal Tracker, Shortcut, Trello, GitHub, and GitLab
Backed by Tempo, giving it continued investment after the acquisition
cons: The Roadmunk brand has been folded into Tempo as 'Strategic Roadmaps (Roadmunk),' and roadmunk.com now redirects to Tempo's site
Two-way Jira/Azure DevOps sync is an extra add-on on the Business plan ($9/collaborator/month) rather than included
Enterprise pricing is custom and requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Is Roadmunk still a separate product?
faqs.answer: Roadmunk has been rebranded as 'Strategic Roadmaps (Roadmunk)' under Tempo, and roadmunk.com now redirects to Tempo's website.

faqs.question: How much does Roadmunk/Strategic Roadmaps cost?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $19/editor/month, Business is $49/editor/month, and Professional is $99/editor/month, all billed annually. Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Does it offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial provides access to Business-tier features, minus API tokens.

faqs.question: What integrations does it support?
faqs.answer: It integrates with Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, Monday.com, Pivotal Tracker, Shortcut, Trello, GitHub, and GitLab, plus SSO.

use_cases.title: Cross-team dependency visibility
use_cases.description: Product and engineering teams track dependencies between projects across a shared roadmap.

use_cases.title: Idea-to-roadmap workflow
use_cases.description: Teams capture and prioritize ideas in the Idea Manager before promoting them onto the roadmap.

use_cases.title: Executive-ready roadmap presentations
use_cases.description: Teams build boardroom-ready timeline and swimlane roadmaps for stakeholder updates.

features: One-way sync with Jira, Azure DevOps, and Trello
Optional two-way Jira/Azure DevOps sync at $9/collaborator/month

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Roadmunk?
paragraphs: Roadmunk is a product roadmapping platform for creating and sharing timeline, swimlane, and table-based roadmaps, alongside an Idea Manager for prioritizing a separate backlog. It has been acquired by Tempo and is now marketed as 'Strategic Roadmaps (Roadmunk),' with roadmunk.com redirecting to Tempo's website.

Paid plans run from a $19/editor/month Starter tier to a $99/editor/month Professional tier (both billed annually), plus custom Enterprise pricing. A 14-day free trial gives access to Business-tier features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Roadmunk is for
paragraphs: Roadmunk is aimed at product managers and cross-functional teams that need dependency tracking and multiple roadmap visualizations, now positioned within Tempo's broader Jira-focused product suite.

enrichment.short_description: SharpCloud, part of Lumivero, is a strategic portfolio management platform connecting data sources into interactive visual decision views.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SharpCloud is a strategic portfolio management platform, part of Lumivero, with interactive visualizations, dependency mapping, and scenarios.

features.title: Data integration
features.description: Connects portfolio, project, and risk data across existing business systems without replacing them.

features.title: Interactive visualizations
features.description: Offers timeline, grid, radar, and kanban views for exploring complex portfolio data.

features.title: Dependency mapping
features.description: Shows how initiatives, risks, and outcomes interconnect.

features.title: Scenario modeling
features.description: Supports what-if analysis and trade-off evaluation across the portfolio.

features.title: Automated data sync
features.description: Scheduled synchronization from source systems such as Excel, SharePoint, Microsoft Project, Azure DevOps, and Jira.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Available as SaaS on Microsoft Azure (US, UK, EU, and government cloud regions) or on-premises/private cloud on Windows or Linux/Docker.

pros: Used by enterprise and government clients including Network Rail and the UK Ministry of Defence, per the company's client list
Supports both SaaS and on-premises/private cloud deployment
SAML 2.0 SSO integration with Azure Active Directory and Okta
Connects to a wide range of existing enterprise systems (Excel, SharePoint, Microsoft Project, Azure DevOps, Jira, SQL databases) rather than requiring data migration
cons: Pricing is not published; all three license tiers (Creator, Collaborator, Read-Only) require contacting sales
Minimum license counts apply per tier (e.g., minimum 50 Read-Only licenses)
No SharpCloud-specific AI features were disclosed on its product page

faqs.question: What license tiers does SharpCloud offer?
faqs.answer: SharpCloud offers Creator (minimum 2 licenses), Collaborator (minimum 5 licenses), and Read-Only (minimum 50 licenses) tiers, all billed annually.

faqs.question: Is SharpCloud cloud-based or on-premises?
faqs.answer: Both. SharpCloud is available as SaaS on Microsoft Azure across US, UK, EU, and government cloud regions, or as an on-premises/private cloud deployment on Windows or Linux/Docker.

faqs.question: Who owns SharpCloud?
faqs.answer: SharpCloud is owned by Lumivero and remains branded as SharpCloud within Lumivero's Risk & Decision software suite.

faqs.question: What industries use SharpCloud?
faqs.answer: SharpCloud's stated clients span aerospace and defense, energy, manufacturing, financial services, and government, including Amcor, Network Rail, and the UK Ministry of Defence.

use_cases.title: Strategic portfolio management
use_cases.description: Enterprises consolidate portfolio, project, and risk data from multiple systems into one interactive view.
use_cases.audience: Portfolio and program managers at large enterprises

use_cases.title: Risk and dependency visualization
use_cases.description: Teams map dependencies between initiatives, risks, and outcomes to support decision-making.

use_cases.title: Regulated or on-premises deployments
use_cases.description: Government and defense organizations deploy SharpCloud on-premises or in private cloud where SaaS isn't permitted.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SharpCloud?
paragraphs: SharpCloud is a strategic portfolio management platform, now part of Lumivero, that connects data from existing business systems into a single interactive environment for visualizing portfolios, mapping dependencies, and running scenario analysis.

It's deployed either as SaaS on Microsoft Azure across several regions or on-premises/private cloud on Windows or Linux/Docker, and licensed through three annually billed tiers: Creator, Collaborator, and Read-Only.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SharpCloud is for
paragraphs: SharpCloud targets large enterprises and government/defense organizations managing complex portfolios, particularly those in aerospace, energy, manufacturing, and financial services who need to connect existing systems rather than replace them.

enrichment.short_description: ProductPlan is a product management platform combining visual roadmapping with AI-moderated customer research surveys.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ProductPlan combines visual roadmapping with AI-moderated research surveys and a Research Agent, used by 1,000+ companies. Pricing via demo.

features.title: Visual timeline roadmaps
features.description: Builds roadmaps designed to be readable across the whole organization, not just the product team.

features.title: AI-moderated research
features.description: Runs customer research surveys with AI-generated adaptive follow-up questions.

features.title: Research Agent
features.description: An AI agent that answers questions about customer research data in plain language.

features.title: Portfolio visibility
features.description: Shows roadmaps across multiple products and teams in one view.

features.title: Jira integration
features.description: Native integration with Jira to align roadmap plans with engineering execution.

features.title: Prioritization frameworks
features.description: Includes customizable frameworks for prioritizing roadmap items.

features.title: Milestone-based launch planning
features.description: Plans product launches around key milestones.

pros: Unlimited viewers are included at no extra cost; pricing is based only on active editors
AI-moderated research surveys automatically synthesize responses into structured insights
Stated scale: 1,000+ customers, 23M+ initiatives planned, and 860K roadmaps built
4.3/5 rating on G2, per the company's own site
cons: No public pricing; all tiers require booking a demo
No free trial or free plan mentioned
Native integration is limited to Jira, with other tools requiring the API

faqs.question: How is ProductPlan priced?
faqs.answer: ProductPlan uses a seat-based model charging only for editors who build and manage roadmaps; viewers are included at no additional cost. Specific per-seat pricing isn't published and requires a demo.

faqs.question: Does ProductPlan offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No free trial is mentioned on ProductPlan's site; pricing details require booking a demo.

faqs.question: What is the Research Agent?
faqs.answer: It's an AI feature that lets users ask questions about customer research data and get answers in plain language.

faqs.question: Does ProductPlan integrate with Jira?
faqs.answer: Yes, ProductPlan has a native Jira integration to keep roadmap plans aligned with engineering work, plus an API for other integrations.

faqs.question: How many customers use ProductPlan?
faqs.answer: ProductPlan states it has more than 1,000 customers, with over 23 million initiatives planned and 860,000 roadmaps built on the platform.

use_cases.title: Organization-wide roadmap communication
use_cases.description: Product teams share visual timeline roadmaps that non-technical stakeholders can understand.

use_cases.title: Evidence-based prioritization
use_cases.description: Teams run AI-moderated customer research surveys and feed synthesized insights directly into roadmap decisions.

use_cases.title: Cross-team portfolio visibility
use_cases.description: Organizations with 3 to 20+ product managers use ProductPlan to see roadmaps across multiple products and teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ProductPlan?
paragraphs: ProductPlan is a product management platform that pairs visual, timeline-based roadmapping with AI-moderated customer research. Its Research Agent lets users ask questions about customer data in plain language, and survey responses are automatically synthesized into structured insights.

The company reports more than 1,000 customers and 860,000 roadmaps built on the platform. Pricing is seat-based, charging only for editors while viewers are unlimited and free, but specific rates are not published and require a demo.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ProductPlan is for
paragraphs: ProductPlan is aimed at product teams ranging from 3 to 20+ product managers across industries like SaaS, insurance, fintech, retail, manufacturing, and government, who need both roadmapping and customer research in one platform.

enrichment.short_description: Portfoleon is portfolio planning software for prioritizing projects against OKRs with timeline, spreadsheet, Kanban, and pivot table views.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Portfoleon is portfolio planning software with OKRs, scenario planning, and multiple views, priced at $48/user/month with a free trial.

features.title: Weighted OKRs
features.description: Set objectives and key results with weighted importance to guide project prioritization.

features.title: Project prioritization
features.description: Prioritizes projects based on their contribution to strategic outcomes.

features.title: Scenario planning
features.description: Supports multiple plan versions for what-if scenario comparisons.

features.title: Multiple visualization boards
features.description: Includes timeline, spreadsheet, Kanban, and pivot table views of the same portfolio data.

features.title: Resource allocation
features.description: Identifies resource bottlenecks across the portfolio.

features.title: Progress monitoring
features.description: Tracks progress throughout the execution cycle.

pros: Only editor accounts are billed; unlimited free read-only accounts are included
Multiple view types (timeline, spreadsheet, Kanban, pivot table) built on the same underlying data
Free trial available via sign-up
Integrates with Zapier, Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, Mailchimp, Slack, and Trello
Perpetual, self-hosted licensing option available under the Enterprise plan
cons: Run by a small team, which may limit support responsiveness compared to larger vendors
No AI capabilities were disclosed on the site
Enterprise pricing is custom and requires contacting sales

faqs.question: How much does Portfoleon cost?
faqs.answer: The Portfolio plan costs $48/user/month billed monthly, with a discounted annual option saving 20%. Enterprise pricing is custom, including a perpetual self-hosted option.

faqs.question: Does Portfoleon offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available through sign-up.

faqs.question: Are read-only accounts free on Portfoleon?
faqs.answer: Yes, Portfoleon offers unlimited free read-only accounts; only editor accounts require a paid subscription.

faqs.question: What integrations does Portfoleon support?
faqs.answer: Portfoleon integrates with Zapier, Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, Mailchimp, Slack, and Trello, and offers an API.

use_cases.title: OKR-driven project prioritization
use_cases.description: Teams weight objectives and key results to prioritize which projects to fund and staff.

use_cases.title: Scenario comparison
use_cases.description: Planners build multiple plan versions to compare trade-offs before committing resources.

use_cases.title: Resource bottleneck identification
use_cases.description: Teams use resource allocation views to spot where capacity is constrained across the portfolio.

features: Unlimited portfolios, boards, and custom fields

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Portfoleon?
paragraphs: Portfoleon is portfolio planning software built to turn strategy into action by weighting OKRs and prioritizing projects based on their contribution to those outcomes. It offers timeline, spreadsheet, Kanban, and pivot table views of the same portfolio data, plus scenario planning across multiple plan versions.
Pricing is per editor at $48/user/month, with unlimited free read-only accounts included, and an Enterprise tier that adds a perpetual, self-hosted licensing option.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Portfoleon is for
paragraphs: Portfoleon is aimed at portfolio and strategy teams that need to connect OKRs to project prioritization and compare multiple planning scenarios before committing resources.

enrichment.short_description: Zeda.io is an AI-powered product discovery platform that centralizes customer feedback to generate insights and prioritize roadmaps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Zeda.io is an AI product discovery platform for centralizing feedback and prioritizing roadmaps, starting at $499/month with a 14-day trial.

features.title: Centralized Voice of Customer
features.description: Collects feedback from Slack, Intercom, surveys, interviews, and other sources into one place.

features.title: AI feedback analysis
features.description: Analyzes incoming feedback automatically and generates insight reports.

features.title: Ask AI
features.description: A generative AI tool for testing hypotheses against collected feedback data.

features.title: Opportunity Radar
features.description: Uses AI to predictively identify emerging issues and opportunities.

features.title: Impact-first roadmaps
features.description: Builds roadmaps and OKRs prioritized by revenue and customer impact.

features.title: In-app feedback widgets and portals
features.description: Collects feedback directly in-product and through public or private customer portals.

features.title: 5,000+ integrations via Zapier
features.description: Connects to CRM, communication, and analytics tools through Zapier.

pros: 14-day free trial available
90-day prorated refund guarantee if unsatisfied
AI-powered insight generation and an 'Ask AI' natural-language query tool over feedback data
Offers migration support from competing tools including ProductBoard, Aha!, Airfocus, UserVoice, and Canny
Notable stated clients include Microsoft, Twilio, PayPal, and Google
cons: No monthly billing option; the Standard plan requires an annual commitment
Starting price of $499/month is higher than many competitors' entry-level plans
Custom/demo-based pricing beyond the published Standard plan is not detailed publicly

faqs.question: How much does Zeda.io cost?
faqs.answer: Zeda.io's Standard plan starts at $499/month, billed annually. There is no monthly billing option.

faqs.question: Does Zeda.io offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available.

faqs.question: Does Zeda.io offer refunds?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zeda.io states a 90-day refund guarantee with a prorated refund if you're unsatisfied.

faqs.question: What is Ask AI?
faqs.answer: Ask AI is a generative AI tool in Zeda.io that lets teams test hypotheses and query feedback data in natural language.

faqs.question: Why doesn't Zeda.io offer monthly billing?
faqs.answer: Zeda.io states its yearly commitment is meant to align both companies toward a longer-term partnership rather than a month-to-month arrangement.

use_cases.title: Centralizing scattered customer feedback
use_cases.description: Teams pull feedback from Slack, Intercom, surveys, and interviews into one system instead of tracking it manually.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted opportunity discovery
use_cases.description: Product teams use Opportunity Radar and Ask AI to surface emerging issues before they escalate.
use_cases.audience: Product managers at SaaS and B2B companies

use_cases.title: Revenue-aligned roadmapping
use_cases.description: Teams prioritize roadmap items using impact-first scoring tied to revenue and OKRs.
use_cases.audience: Product leaders focused on business impact

features: AI product insights and validation tools

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Zeda.io?
paragraphs: Zeda.io is an AI-powered product discovery platform that centralizes Voice of Customer data from sources like Slack, Intercom, surveys, and interviews, then uses AI to generate insight reports and predict emerging opportunities through its Opportunity Radar feature.

Its Standard plan starts at $499/month, billed annually only, with a 14-day free trial and a 90-day prorated refund guarantee. The company reports clients including Microsoft, Twilio, PayPal, and Google.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Zeda.io is for
paragraphs: Zeda.io is aimed at product, customer success, sales, and marketing teams at SaaS and B2B companies that need to turn scattered customer feedback into prioritized, revenue-aligned roadmaps.

name: Chisel
skip_reason: Official domain chisellabs.com does not resolve (DNS lookup failure confirmed independently via WebFetch, curl, and nslookup on both the apex and www subdomains); the site is currently unreachable so facts could not be verified from the official source.
